title: About FermatMind
summary: FermatMind is a self-understanding and growth platform that turns assessments, career exploration, and decision support into a system people can use over time.
contentMd:
## Who we are

FermatMind is a self-understanding and growth platform for students, early-career professionals, career changers, and long-term builders. We turn assessment results into clearer language for decisions, communication, learning, and career exploration.

## Why we exist

Most people are not starting from zero. They already sense their preferences, stress points, strengths, and tradeoffs, but the information is scattered. FermatMind helps convert those signals into structured hypotheses that can be discussed, tested, and reviewed over time.

## How we build

We combine measurement, interpretation, action planning, and review. A result should not stop at a label; it should help users understand patterns, test choices, and improve future decisions.

## Our boundaries

FermatMind does not provide medical diagnosis, emergency care, hiring decisions, school admission decisions, or legal advice. Our tools support self-observation and planning, and high-risk decisions should involve qualified professionals.

title: FermatMind Public-Benefit Giving Plan
summary: An independent public-benefit giving plan from FermatMind. Beginning June 1, 2026, we plan to publish daily giving records and make clear that this activity does not imply a formal relationship, certification, affiliation, approval, or endorsement by the United Nations Foundation or the United Nations.
metaDescription: An independent public-benefit giving plan from FermatMind. Beginning June 1, 2026, we plan to publish daily giving records and make clear that this activity does not imply a formal relationship, certification, affiliation, approval, or endorsement by the United Nations Foundation or the United Nations.
contentMd:
## Hero

FermatMind is turning its long-term interest in youth growth, structured decision support, and public-interest issues into a transparent daily action.

Beginning June 1, 2026, FermatMind plans to make a daily public-benefit donation and publish a record of that donation on its website and social channels. The current recipient organization is the United Nations Foundation.

This page records FermatMind’s independent giving plan. It does not imply endorsement, certification, a formal relationship, affiliation, authorization, or approval by the United Nations Foundation or the United Nations.

## Why We Publish This

FermatMind is not only about a single assessment result. Our work is focused on helping young people build clearer self-understanding, career exploration, and long-term decision capacity.

The public-benefit giving plan is one way to turn that long-term responsibility into a visible record. We want this action to remain transparent, reviewable, and bounded, without presenting it as an unverified legal entity, external relationship, or third-party endorsement.

This plan does not change FermatMind’s assessment logic, report generation, privacy policy, or user data handling.

## Recipient Organization

Current recipient organization: United Nations Foundation.

The United Nations Foundation supports the United Nations and works on global issues connected to the Sustainable Development Goals. FermatMind’s current giving plan is associated with the United Nations Foundation because its mission is aligned with our long-term interest in youth growth, public-interest issues, and responsible decision support.

This is FermatMind’s own independent giving activity. It should not be interpreted as endorsement, certification, a formal relationship, affiliation, authorization, approval, or fundraising activity for the United Nations Foundation or the United Nations.

## Latest Records Placeholder

Beginning June 1, 2026, FermatMind plans to publish daily records after each public-benefit donation is completed. Records may include the date, recipient organization, donation status, proof status, and social update links.

To protect payment security and personal privacy, any public proof materials will redact payment accounts, full transaction numbers, email addresses, phone numbers, addresses, and other sensitive information.

The full daily giving record system will be launched in a later version.

## Full Archive Entrypoint

The full archive entrypoint will be available after the Daily Giving Ledger is launched. A later version is planned to include a daily record index and monthly archive pages for published giving records.

Before the archive system is available, this page only provides the program explanation, boundary note, and future record entrypoint notice.

## Social Updates

After each daily donation is completed, FermatMind plans to publish a corresponding social update and link back to the official website record.

The website record remains the primary reference. Social screenshots, links, or proof notes do not change the boundary of this page: this is FermatMind’s independent giving record and does not imply endorsement, certification, a formal relationship, affiliation, authorization, or approval by the recipient organization or the United Nations.

## Important Boundary Note

The phrase “public-benefit giving plan” refers to FermatMind’s own independent donation record. It does not mean FermatMind has created a separate public-benefit legal entity or registered public-benefit body.

Unless this page explicitly discloses formal legal entity details in the future, this plan should not be interpreted as a completed legal public-benefit entity.

This plan also does not imply endorsement, certification, a formal relationship, affiliation, authorization, approval, or sponsorship by the United Nations Foundation or the United Nations.

FermatMind is not collecting funds for the United Nations Foundation. Use of FermatMind products should not be described as a direct user donation to the United Nations Foundation.

## FAQ

### Q1: What is the FermatMind Public-Benefit Giving Plan?

It is FermatMind’s independent giving plan. Beginning June 1, 2026, FermatMind plans to make a daily public-benefit donation and publish a record on its website and social channels.

### Q2: Does this mean FermatMind has created a separate public-benefit legal entity?

No. Unless this page explicitly discloses formal legal entity details in the future, this plan should not be interpreted as a separate public-benefit legal entity, registered body, or public-benefit status.

### Q3: Does FermatMind have a formal relationship with the United Nations Foundation or the United Nations?

No. This page records FermatMind’s independent giving activity. It does not imply endorsement, certification, a formal relationship, affiliation, authorization, sponsorship, or approval by the United Nations Foundation or the United Nations.

### Q4: Are the donations restricted to a specific designated program?

Unless a future record explicitly identifies a specific donation designation, this page should not be read as claiming that donations are restricted to a specific project, region, team, or program.

### Q5: Does completing a FermatMind assessment directly trigger a donation?

No. The giving plan is independently operated by FermatMind. User actions should not be described as directly triggering a specific donation, and FermatMind is not collecting funds for the United Nations Foundation.

### Q6: How can the records be reviewed?

FermatMind plans to publish records on its website and social channels. The full daily giving record system will be launched in a later version. Public proof materials will redact payment and personal-sensitive information.

title: Work With FermatMind
summary: A cautious overview of the kinds of work FermatMind may need over time, without announcing current openings, hiring timelines, employment terms, or an active recruiting process.
metaDescription: A cautious overview of the kinds of work FermatMind may need over time, without announcing current openings, hiring timelines, employment terms, or an active recruiting process.
contentMd:
# Work With FermatMind

## About this page
This page describes the kinds of work FermatMind may need as the product develops. It does not announce current job openings, guarantee hiring, describe employment terms, or create a recruiting process. Official opportunities, if available, should be confirmed only through current official channels.

## The work we care about
FermatMind works on assessment interpretation, career exploration, product systems, content quality, and user-facing decision support. The work requires careful language, product judgment, engineering reliability, privacy awareness, and respect for the limits of assessment tools.

## Possible collaboration areas
Depending on future needs, relevant areas may include research and methodology, content and editing, product design, user research, frontend and backend engineering, data analysis, quality assurance, operations, and responsible growth. This list is descriptive and does not mean any role is currently open.

## What we value in collaborators
We value clarity, careful reasoning, respect for human complexity, strong execution, and willingness to revise work when evidence changes. We also value sensitivity to privacy, psychological safety, and high-risk settings where assessment results can be misused.

## Future contact and official channels
If FermatMind opens formal roles or collaboration channels, the relevant information should appear through official public channels. Until then, this page should be read as a directional overview, not as an invitation, job advertisement, hiring promise, or application instruction.

title: Privacy Policy
summary: This privacy baseline explains what data FermatMind may process, why it is processed, and how users can manage privacy requests.
contentMd:
## Information we process

FermatMind may process account information, assessment responses, generated reports, order records, device and log data, and support messages when users interact with the service.

## How we use information

We use information to provide assessments and reports, maintain product reliability, process purchases, prevent abuse, improve user experience, and meet legal or operational obligations.

## User choices

Users may request access, correction, deletion, or other privacy support through the available support channels. Some records may need to be retained when required for security, accounting, dispute handling, or legal compliance.

## Contact

For privacy questions, contact FermatMind through the support channel listed on the site.

title: Terms of Use
summary: These terms describe the baseline rules for using FermatMind products, reports, content, and paid services.
contentMd:
## Use of service

FermatMind provides assessment, interpretation, career exploration, and growth support products. Users are responsible for using the service lawfully and for keeping account access secure.

## Assessment boundaries

Reports and recommendations are informational tools. They are not medical diagnosis, therapy, emergency support, hiring authority, admission authority, or a substitute for qualified professional judgment.

## Paid services

Some features may require payment. Product access, refunds, and support handling follow the policies presented at the time of purchase and in related policy pages.

## Changes

We may update products, policies, or terms as the service evolves. Material updates should be reflected in the published content page metadata.

title: Assessment Method and Boundaries
summary: How to use FermatMind assessment results responsibly, what they can support, and where their limits begin.
contentMd:
## What FermatMind assessments are

FermatMind assessments are structured self-understanding tools. They collect responses, organize them through defined scoring or interpretation models, and turn the result into language that can help people reflect on personality patterns, interests, work preferences, and growth questions.

A result is best read as a working hypothesis about tendencies in a specific context. It is not a permanent identity, a full biography, or a complete measure of a person's ability, character, health, future, or worth.

## What results can support

Assessment results can help with self-observation, communication, career exploration, learning plans, team conversations, and personal review. They can make patterns easier to name, compare, and discuss.

Results are most useful when combined with real-world evidence: past choices, work samples, feedback from trusted people, repeated behavior over time, and the user's current constraints. They should help users ask better questions and plan next steps, not remove judgment from the user.

## What results cannot decide

FermatMind results do not guarantee school admission, employment, promotion, career success, relationship outcomes, income, health outcomes, or any other high-stakes result. They should not be used as the sole basis for hiring, firing, promotion, compensation, admission, financial decisions, medical decisions, legal decisions, or disciplinary action.

Scores, types, labels, and recommended paths can be incomplete or wrong for a particular person. Human development is affected by environment, opportunity, training, health, relationships, incentives, and time. A report cannot capture all of those conditions.

## Medical and high-stakes limits

FermatMind is not a medical diagnosis, psychological diagnosis, therapy service, crisis service, emergency service, or substitute for qualified professional care. Assessment output should not be treated as a diagnosis of depression, anxiety, ADHD, personality disorder, neurodevelopmental condition, or any other health condition.

If a user is in acute distress, has thoughts of self-harm, experiences serious symptoms, or faces a safety risk, they should seek help from local emergency services, licensed clinicians, trusted family members, or other qualified support instead of relying on an online assessment.

## Data and privacy boundaries

Assessment responses, reports, orders, support messages, device logs, and account information may be processed to provide the service, keep it reliable, handle orders, respond to support requests, protect security, and improve product quality. Privacy handling follows the published Privacy Policy and the notices shown in product flows.

Users should avoid entering unnecessary sensitive information into free-text fields. Organizations should not request or expose individual results unless the purpose, authorization, visibility, retention, appeal path, and privacy obligations are clear.

## How to use results responsibly

Use results as structured input for reflection. Start with one or two claims that feel useful, compare them with real behavior, and test a small next step for a defined period. Keep what is useful, challenge what does not fit, and review the result again when circumstances change.

For education, career, clinical, legal, financial, or employment decisions, combine any assessment insight with qualified human judgment, relevant professional standards, direct evidence, and the user's own goals. The assessment is a tool for thinking, not an authority that decides for the user.
